  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Temporary bracing removed before decking or permanent stability elements installed",
    "Structure lacks required lateral stability per OSHA 1926 Subpart R — columns and beams not adequately braced against collapse",
    "Erection sequence non-compliant: permanent framing connections or decking not in place before bracing removal",
    "Installed members classified defective due to unsafe stability condition"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately reinstall temporary bracing or guy cables to restore lateral stability",
    "Stop further work in the affected area until bracing is restored and inspected",
    "Install decking or permanent lateral stability elements before removing any temporary bracing",
    "Verify all bolted connections are properly tightened to specified torque before considering bracing removal",
    "Conduct structural stability inspection per AISC 303 tolerances before proceeding"
  ],
  "rationale": "Temporary bracing was prematurely removed before decking or permanent stability elements were installed, creating an immediate collapse hazard. OSHA 1926 Subpart R requires that structural framing be adequately braced at all times during erection, and bracing must remain in place until permanent connections or decking provide stability. This is a critical safety violation requiring immediate corrective action. The work is roughly 70 percent complete but cannot advance until the stability defect is corrected.",
  "references": ["AISC 303", "OSHA 1926 Subpart R"]
}
